entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: The primary cereal crop analyzed in the Ethiopian agricultural systems.

entities.name: NITROGEN USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to utilize nitrogen effectively for growth and yield.

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the synthesis report's analyzed studies were conducted.

entities.name: NATIONAL WHEAT SELF-SUFFICIENCY INITIATIVE
entities.description: A national program aimed at increasing wheat production to achieve self-sufficiency.

entities.name: NPS FERTILIZERS
entities.description: Fertilizers containing Nitrogen, Phosphorus, and Sulfur used to improve crop productivity.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing crop growth and soil health through various agricultural techniques.

entities.name: FERTILIZER RATE TRIALS
entities.description: Experimental studies conducted to determine the optimal amount of fertilizer for maximum yield.

entities.name: INTEGRATED NUTRIENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: A holistic approach to managing soil fertility using a combination of organic and inorganic nutrient sources.

entities.name: CLIMATE
entities.description: Environmental factors that influence crop growth and nitrogen utilization.

entities.name: LANDSCAPE VARIABILITY
entities.description: The physical differences in terrain and soil across a geographic area.

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC FACTORS
entities.description: Social and economic variables that influence farming decisions and practices.

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil common in Ethiopia where NUE research is focused.

entities.name: NITISOLS
entities.description: A type of reddish-brown soil common in Ethiopia where NUE research is focused.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A metric used to measure the increase in yield per unit of nitrogen applied.

entities.name: PARTIAL FACTOR PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: A metric measuring the yield increase per unit of a specific input, such as nitrogen.

entities.name: RECOVERY EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A metric measuring the proportion of applied nitrogen taken up by the plant.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A metric measuring the yield increase per unit of nitrogen absorbed by the plant.

entities.name: INTERNAL EFFICIENCY
entities.description: A metric measuring the yield increase per unit of nitrogen present in the plant tissue.

entities.name: FARMER PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The economic gain achieved by farmers through improved agricultural efficiency.
